description_long: This is a two-story bank building with a prominent terracotta-tiled gable roof and a symmetrical facade. The front features a large, arched double door framed by decorative columns and gold accents, flanked by two windows with ornate trim and sills. The upper story has two additional windows, each with matching decorative elements, and a row of small gold embellishments beneath the roofline. The base of the building is finished with a stone texture, adding a sense of solidity and security. The side and rear walls are plain, lacking windows or ornamentation, while the roof extends uniformly across the structure. The overall design suggests a neoclassical influence, emphasizing formality and stability.
description: A two-story neoclassical bank with a terracotta-tiled gable roof, ornate front entrance, and decorative window trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched double door, four windows with ornate trim, gold accents, and stone base.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain wall with no windows or decoration, and the sloped terracotta roof.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a plain wall, a small utility box, and the back of the arched entrance, with the roof extending over.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with a plain wall and the continuation of the tiled roof.
attribute_entrance_design: One large arched double door made of wood with gold decorative trim, framed by two columns and ornate molding.
